description: Issue filed from a Slack-like thread where the immediate message references earlier context distributed across the surrounding thread. Provider-neutral: Slack is one adapter instance, not the schema (axis: external-source-provider).

thread.author: PM
thread.text: managed/dynamic progress tips should be real, not hard-coded copy with anti-repeat behavior

thread.author: PM
thread.text: also the raw timestamp needs a shared time-rendering requirement, not a one-off fix

thread.author: PM
thread.text: and the stop/terminate/cancel/restart control authority questions are still unclear

thread.author: Eng
thread.text: filing an issue for the timestamp thing

why_it_matters: The immediate message (p4) only references the timestamp; the broader product intent lives in p1-p3. Preserving only the immediate paraphrase loses the thread's intent — the downstream-waiter fallout this contract prevents.
created_body_source_text:
## Problem

Raw timestamp rendering needs a shared time-rendering requirement.

## Source

- access: private-grant (slack-bot)
- freshness: 2026-06-07T02:14Z
- Source preservation: source-text
- Source text: |
    > p1: managed/dynamic progress tips should be real, not hard-coded copy
    > p2: the raw timestamp needs a shared time-rendering requirement
    > p3: stop/terminate/cancel/restart control authority is unclear
